channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: New York City mayor meets with Trump's 'border czar' to discuss how to go after 'violent' criminals
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams met with President-elect Donald Trump's incoming “border czar” on Thursday, with the Democratic mayor expressing an enthusiasm to work with the incoming administration to pursue violent criminals in the city while Trump promises mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE and CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump invited China's Xi to his inauguration even as he threatened massive tariffs on Beijing
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has nvited Chinese President Xi Jinping to attend his inauguration next month — extending a diplomatic olive branch even as Trump threatens to levy massive tariffs on Chinese goods.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 3 men say in lawsuits that Sean ‘Diddy’ Combs drugged and sexually assaulted them
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Three men sued Sean “Diddy” Combs in New York on Thursday, claiming the hip-hop mogul drugged and raped them.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Justice Department, Louisville reach deal after probe prompted by Breonna Taylor killing
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — The U.S. Justice Department and the city of Louisville have reached an agreement to reform the city’s police force after an investigation prompted by the fatal police shooting of Breonna Taylor and police treatment of protesters, officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: DYLAN LOVAN and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Family of Ellie Young files lawsuit against accused killer and his mother
item.description: Nearly two months after the killing, Young's family has filed a wrongful death lawsuit against the alleged shooter and his mother, Martha Hopper.
item.dc:creator: Megan Fayard, NewsNation

item.title: Coffee prices hit record high after challenging growing season
item.description: Higher coffee prices could be brewing for consumers as the global coffee market smashed a 47-year record.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, KRON4

item.title: Proposed 'Trump' law seeks to allow felons to run for office in Missouri
item.description: A Missouri representative is proposing a repeal of a state law that currently prevents citizens with a felony history from running for a state office.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mueller, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mysterious drone flights over New Jersey: Homeland Security shares new details
item.description: The large mysterious drones reported flying over parts of New Jersey in recent weeks appear to avoid detection by traditional methods such as helicopter and radio, according to a state lawmaker briefed Wednesday by the Department of Homeland Security.
item.dc:creator: Bruce Shipkowski, KRON4

item.title: American who says he crossed into Syria by foot is free after 7 months
item.description: An American who turned up in Syria on Thursday says he was detained after crossing into the country by foot on a Christian pilgrimage seven months ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Bill Clinton says he’s open to talking to Biden about preemptive pardon for Hillary Clinton
item.description: Former President Bill Clinton said he is open to having a conversation with President Joe Biden about a potential preemptive pardon for his wife, former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Teacher sues Beverly Hills school district for racism after Trump win
item.description: The lawyer told NewsNation there were several specific incidents, including a video showing students mocking Black students in the school cafeteria.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Atmospheric river brings weather whiplash to East Coast as bomb cyclone develops
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — The U.S. East Coast began a whiplash-inducing stretch of weather Wednesday with a deluge of rain, rapid snowmelt and powerful gusts, creating dangerous conditions, due in part to an atmospheric river and developing bomb cyclone.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kayaker who faked his death, fled the US charged after returning to Wisconsin
item.description: Police had previously said they believed Borgwardt planned his Aug. 12 disappearance and tried to conceal his efforts to mislead investigations.
item.dc:creator: Indiana Schilz, ABC 4

item.title: Aftermath of small plane crash onto Texas roadway caught on camera with split fuselage, damaged vehicles
item.description: The FAA and the Victoria Police Department in Texas said a small plane crashed into a busy intersection, colliding with three vehicles Wednesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Key details about the man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare's CEO
item.description: The man accused of killing the head of one of the biggest U.S. health insurance companies is fighting being sent to face charges in New York City, where the attack happened.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE and SARAH BRUMFIELD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Will TikTok be banned in January? Here's where things stand as deadline nears
item.description: You may not be able to doomscroll on TikTok much longer unless the social media company finds a way to stop a new law from going into effect.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KTLA 5

item.title: Wanted posters appear in NYC listing healthcare CEOs after shooting
item.description: Videos circulating on social media show wanted posters in New York City with photos of healthcare CEOs a week after the fatal shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson in Midtown.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden says healthy women help US prosperity as he highlights White House initiative on their health
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden said Wednesday that he is “so proud” that a women's health research initiative he launched last year at his wife's urging has already invested nearly $1 billion because a healthy female population improves U.S. prosperity.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Christopher Wray to resign as FBI director ahead of Trump takeover
item.description: FBI Director Christopher Wray announced Wednesday he plans to resign from the agency, leaving his post ahead of President-Trump's apparent plans to fire him.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Garbage truck explodes in Illinois suburb, sending debris flying and injuring first responders
item.description: Two police cameras in a Chicago suburb caught a blast following a garbage truck fire. The cause is under investigation while two police officers are still recovering.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Doing holiday shopping on Amazon? Get your loved one a Hyundai
item.description: You might need a bigger cart. At least that’s what Hyundai says of their newly launched partnership with Amazon, where the South Korean automakers now have cars available for purchase. That’s right: you can buy a real car on Amazon.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump, team trail Fauci on trust as medical information source: Survey
item.description: Trump and other Republicans have criticized Fauci, who was the face of the first Trump and Biden administration’s response against COVID-19, over his handling of the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Dick Van Dyke, Cher among those forced to flee Malibu homes in wake of wildfire
item.description: Officials said the fire, which grew significantly overnight, was only 7% contained as of Wednesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, Fox 5

item.title: Albertsons gives up on Kroger merger, sues the grocery chain
item.description: Kroger and Albertsons in 2022 proposed what would be the largest grocery store merger in U.S. history. The companies said a merger would help them better compete with big retailers like Walmart, Costco and Amazon.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Smokey Robinson and Halle Bailey to host 'A Motown Christmas' special honoring the label's legacy
item.description: UNIVERSAL CITY, Calif. (AP) — Smokey Robinson and Halle Bailey — bridging Motown's old and new school — are lending their voices during a holiday special to honor the groundbreaking record label that changed music forever.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Bernie Sanders drops major hint about political future
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.), one of the Senate’s most prominent progressives, says he likely won’t run again for the Senate after his fourth term expires in 2030. Asked in an interview with Politico whether this would be his last term in Congress, Sanders replied: “I’m 83 now.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KTLA 5

item.title: Daniel Penny defense eyes Manhattan DA Alvin Bragg for potential malicious prosecution lawsuit
item.description: Daniel Penny's defense team is floating the possibility of a malicious prosecution lawsuit against Manhattan District Attorney Alvin Bragg after Penny's acquittal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Your daily cup of coffee could get more expensive because of climate change
item.description: Coffee lovers and café dwellers are likely to see the price of a cup of joe increase soon, experts warn.
item.dc:creator: Maya Eaglin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How to pick the perfect Windows laptop
item.description: Shopping for a new computer can be intimidating. There are so many models to choose from and so many specs to decide on. "They get really excited when they come in here.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Mangione family in Maryland: What we know
item.description: Although many people outside of Maryland came to know the "Mangione" name only after the arrest of Luigi Mangione, 26, who is accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, the family has a number of prominent ties to the state where he grew up.
item.dc:creator: Brian Farrell, Fox 5

item.title: Customer recalls moment Luigi Mangione spotted in Pennsylvania McDonald's
item.description: New details are emerging about what happened at the Altoona, Pennsylvania McDonald's where police arrested Luigi Mangione, the suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare's CEO, on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Las Vegas judge dismisses infamous 'Black Widow' murder case after 27 years
item.description: The judge dismissed murder charges against Margaret Rudin, the woman dubbed "The Black Widow" who a jury convicted of killing her husband more than two decades ago.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, Fox 5

item.title: House task force cites 'various failures' ahead of 'preventable' Trump shooting
item.description: The House select committee that investigated the two assassination attempts against President-elect Trump released a report on Tuesday that found the July 13 attack at Trump’s rally in Butler, Pa., was “preventable and should not have happened.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WGN 9

item.title: Still rockin': As singer turns 80, the Christmas song she sang as a teen is a holiday staple
item.description: Her memories of recording “Rockin' Around the Christmas Tree” are a little hazy. She remembers the producer placing decorations around the studio and blasting the air conditioner on a warm Nashville day to create a holiday spirit. The musicians, she recalls, nailed it in a couple of takes.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER AP Media Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Amazon offering $25 flights for Prime members — but only for one eligible group
item.description: The deal, which runs through Friday, aims to offer 1,000 bookings per day for a total of 5,000 $25 fares.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5
